title: VerilatedFstSc inherits final VerilatedFst
body:
I'm currently trying to generate traces with systemc. Unfortunatly the class `VerilatedFstSc` inherits the **final** class `VerilatedFst`. This is a ill-formed statement since final classes can't be inherited.  
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/41f29d2ce397f0c46341bce86650bb7856d22b7d/include/verilated_fst_c.h#L39
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/41f29d2ce397f0c46341bce86650bb7856d22b7d/include/verilated_fst_sc.h#L35

https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/language/final : 

> When used in a class definition, final specifies that this class may not appear in the base-specifier-list of another class definition (in other words, cannot be derived from). The program is ill-formed otherwise.

What OS and distribution are you using?
ubuntu 20.04 and clang-12

Would you be willing to try to fix Verilator yourself with assistance?
yes
